summary: Takotsubo cardiomyopathy must be diagnosed with absence of obstructive coronary disease proven by coronary angiography; without this diagnostic step reported here, it can't be concluded despite strong clinical similarity. This makes the current presentation more consistent with acute stress-induced cardiac dysfunction than an officially recognized condition like takotsubo.
